channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Even America's 250th birthday has grown tumultuous
item.description: The United States is throwing a big 250th birthday party this summer. Planning between two groups has become highly politicized.
item.content:encoded: The United States is throwing a big 250th birthday party this summer. Planning between two groups has become highly politicized.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Putin promotes a new world economic order at St. Petersburg forum
item.description: At "Russian Davos," Putin ruled out meeting with Zelenskyy and promoted a new world economic order.
item.content:encoded: At "Russian Davos," Putin ruled out meeting with Zelenskyy and promoted a new world economic order.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Ex-Border Patrol commander Greg Bovino spoke at an international far-right conference
item.description: Former Border Patrol commander Greg Bovino recently spoke at an international far-right gathering alongside white supremacists and neo-Nazis.
item.content:encoded: Former Border Patrol commander Greg Bovino recently spoke at an international far-right gathering alongside white supremacists and neo-Nazis.
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: New album by the band Red Clay Strays has this reviewer feeling grateful
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Laura Grant of station WEXT in Albany, N.Y., about new music out Friday by the Alabama-based band The Red Clay Strays.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Laura Grant of station WEXT in Albany, N.Y., about new music out Friday by the Alabama-based band The Red Clay Strays.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: GLP-1s might reduce risk of cancer. Researchers are asking why
item.description: There is mounting evidence to suggest GLP-1 drugs designed as diabetes and obesity treatments also help reduce cancer risk.
item.content:encoded: There is mounting evidence to suggest GLP-1 drugs designed as diabetes and obesity treatments also help reduce cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: CDC report: Ebola outbreak could rival the worst on record unless world acts
item.description: New modeling from the CDC shows that if measures aren't taken immediately, this outbreak could sicken more than 20,000 people in the next three months.
item.content:encoded:
New modeling from the CDC shows that if measures aren't taken immediately, this outbreak could sicken more than 20,000 people in the next three months.

(Image credit: Daniel Buuma)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: In the wake of layoffs, some tech workers are exploring new careers
item.description: Amid widespread tech layoffs, some highly skilled workers are making radical career changes. Some laid-off workers are turning to lower paying temp jobs, and some are leaving tech altogether.
item.content:encoded: Amid widespread tech layoffs, some highly skilled workers are making radical career changes. Some laid-off workers are turning to lower paying temp jobs, and some are leaving tech altogether.
item.dc:creator: Monica Nickelsburg

item.title: Staff told to remove President Trump from the Kennedy Center's name
item.description: President Trump's name is coming off the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's name is coming off the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair

item.title: Maya Hawke shares the experience of realizing that she wasn't crazy
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Actor and musician Maya Hawke shares the experience of realizing that she wasn't crazy.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Actor and musician Maya Hawke shares the experience of realizing that she wasn't crazy.

item.title: What teeth enamel tells us about ancient human diets
item.description: The enamel on our teeth is the hardest tissue on the human body. A new study looks at the nanoscale structure of enamel from teeth dating as far back as 18 million years ago to see how it's changed.
item.content:encoded: The enamel on our teeth is the hardest tissue on the human body. A new study looks at the nanoscale structure of enamel from teeth dating as far back as 18 million years ago to see how it's changed.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott
